title: Senior Software Engineer, £70,000 To £85,000, UK
jd_text: What we're buildingLifted is fixing one of the UK's most broken sectors: care. We've built compliance and immigration tech that helps care providers hire both locally and internationally, while automating the painful Home Office requirements, right-to-work checks, and all the paperwork that usually makes this take months. We're now expanding our product surface area. We're not a typical recruiting platform - we're infrastructure for the entire sector.Why this mattersA lot of engineering roles are pretty removed from their impact. This one isn't, we're fixing critical infrastructure in a sector that's struggling. The systems you build have immediate, tangible impact.The roleWe need senior engineers who can own features end-to-end - from understanding the problem through to shipping the solution.What that looks like:You'll work directly with stakeholders to understand what needs buildingYou'll start with feature goals, and have ownership of implementation details through to roll outYou'll make pragmatic scope decisions when trading off speed vs completenessYou'll ship features that directly impact our usersWhat support looks like:You'll work collaboratively with the team - we're building our documentation practices as we scaleYou'll work with the broader team to align on prioritiesYou're not alone, but trusted to drive things forwardThe stack (high level)Backend: Node.js, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, TypeScriptFrontend: SolidJS, TypeScriptInfrastructure: AWS, TerraformTools: Cursor, Linear, Notion, GitHubPreferred: TypeScript & Node.js experienceBut not mandatory: We care more about good engineering judgment and the ability to ship than specific techNice to have: ML/AI experience - we're expanding our AI capabilitiesWhat we're looking forYou should apply if you:Have built and shipped production systems before (we need senior-level judgment)Have experience at the startup-to-scale transition - managing technical debt, building for scale, and keeping things reliable as they growEnjoy talking to stakeholders and understanding the problem spaceWrite code that other people can understand and maintainHave worked remotely and communicate well asyncAre energised by early-stage environments where things are still being figured outLogisticsSalary: £70,000 to £85,000 depending on experienceLocation: Remote (UK based) or hybrid, we have an office in central LondonHours: Full-time/Contractors
arr_raw: Remote (UK based) or hybrid, we have an office in central London
tech_raw: Node.js, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, TypeScript, SolidJS, TypeScript, AWS, Terraform, Cursor, Linear, Notion, GitHub, TypeScript & Node.js, ML/AI
label: good_fit

title: Front End Developer (remote)
jd_text: If you’ve used an App to order food or pay a bill in a restaurant, you may have been one of the millions of users who use our Apps for ordering, paying bills, and earning loyalty rewards. We’re a London-base company building the leading digital hospitality platform, and that means designing and building iOS and Android Apps, management web portals, backend services, e-pos integrations and much more.We have an immediate vacancy for a junior/mid level front-end developer to join our team and help us build best-in-class web applications.The RoleThe roadmap for our web app and our portal web site has many ambitious features; they will be a challenge to implement.This is a hands-on role developing web applications using React.js and Typescript. You’ll be working with your colleagues in the Front End team and the Platform team to architect new features, and build performant and delightful user experiences.We use an agile Kanban process for development. We emphasise planning and design in our process, and aim for fast, continuous delivery.Who Should ApplyWe are looking for developers with 1-3 years’ experience in a commercial team. You’ve worked on real-world websites or applications, know how to problem solve, and you set a high quality standard.In particular we are looking for:experience with React.js and related design patternsexperience in ES6, Typescript and Debugging, Javascript build tools and packagersexperience in web service technologies and knowledge of Node.jsFluent spoken EnglishA university degree in CS, or STEM subjectWhile we embrace the advantages of AI in our workflow, it is expected that interviews will be carried out without the use of such tools.LocationThis role is suitable for remote working. In fact all our developers work remotely, but you do have the option to work in the office if you want to and if you are based in the UK. You will always find Pepper staff in our London office at Shepherds Bush.Job Benefits & PerksRemote / Home Working, or London WeWork optionCompetitive Salary25 Days Holiday + Bank Holiday (rising to 28 days with service ) Stock Options (on promotion)Modern Cloud TechWork on Customer Facing SystemsExperienced Co-Workers Development Best PracticesCareer Advancement OpportunitiesClaude Subscription
arr_raw: This role is suitable for remote working. In fact all our developers work remotely, but you do have the option to work in the office if you want to and if you are based in the UK.
tech_raw: React.js, Typescript, ES6, Debugging, Javascript build tools, packagers, Node.js
label: maybe

title: Founding Engineer (£80k-£100k + Equity) At Chalkie AI
jd_text: This is a job that Jill, our AI Recruiter, is recruiting for on behalf of one of our customers.She will pick the best candidates from Jack's networkThe next step is to speak to Jack.Founding EngineerSalary: £80k-£100k + EquityCompany Description: Chalkie AI - Triple Point Ventures backed EdTech startupJob Description:Join a high-growth startup that scaled from £0 to £2.5M ARR in just six months. As our third founding engineer, you will work directly with experienced founders to build the first AI lesson generation tool teachers actually love. You will own features end-to-end, shaping the technical infrastructure for over 200,000 global users.Location: Remote, UKWhy this role is remarkable:Massive traction with £2.5M ARR and 200k+ users achieved in under six months with a lean, elite team.Direct impact on global education by building tools that save teachers hours of planning time every week.High-autonomy founding role with significant equity and the ability to define the engineering culture from day one.What you will do:Lead full-stack development using NextJS and Laravel to ship production-ready features for 20,000+ weekly active users.Architect and scale technical infrastructure on GCP and Digital Ocean to support millions of future students and teachers.Collaborate daily with the CEO, CTO, and CPO to ideate, problem-solve, and execute the core product vision.The ideal candidate:Proven experience building products from 0-to-1 as a former founder or early-stage startup engineer.Strong full-stack proficiency in PHP/Laravel and React/NextJS with a passion for AI-first development workflows.Product-minded engineer who prioritizes user experience and rapid shipping over perfect code abstraction.Who are Jack & Jill?Ok, I'll go first. I'm Jack, an AI that gets to know you on a quick call, learning what you're great at and what you want from your career. Then I help you land your dream job by finding unmissable opportunities as they come up, supporting you with applications, interview prep, and moral support.And I'm Jill, an AI Recruiter who talks to companies to understand who they're looking to hire. Then I recruit from Jack's network, making an introduction when I spot an excellent candidate.Next stepsStep 1. Visit our website.Step 2. Click 'Talk to Jack'.Step 3. Talk to Jack so he can understand your experience and ambitions.Step 4. Jack will make sure Jill (the AI agent working for the company) considers you for this role.Step 5. If Jill thinks you're a great fit and her client wants to meet you, they will make the introduction.Step 6. If not, Jack will find you excellent alternatives. All for free.We never post fake jobsThis isn't a trick. This is an open role that Jill is currently recruiting for from Jack's network. Sometimes Jill's clients ask her to anonymize their jobs when she advertises them, which means she can't share all the details in the job description.We appreciate this can make them look a bit suspect, but there isn't much we can do about it. Give Jack a spin! You could land this role. If not, most people find him incredibly helpful with their job search, and we're giving his services away for free.
label: good_fit

title: Frontend Platform Engineer
jd_text: Intercom is the AI Customer Service company on a mission to help businesses provide incredible customer experiences.Our AI agent Fin, the most advanced customer service AI agent on the market, lets businesses deliver always-on, impeccable customer service and ultimately transform their customer experiences for the better. Fin can also be combined with our Helpdesk to become a complete solution called the Intercom Customer Service Suite, which provides AI enhanced support for the more complex or high touch queries that require a human agent.Founded in 2011 and trusted by nearly 30,000 global businesses, Intercom is setting the new standard for customer service. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and consistently deliver incredible value to our customers.What's the opportunity?As a Frontend Platform Engineer on our Frontend Platform team at Intercom, you’ll be responsible for defining and evolving the foundations of how Intercom builds user interfaces. Our mission is to make it faster, safer, and easier for product teams to build, iterate, and ship high-quality products at scale.We’re modernizing our frontend stack with React, and this is your chance to set the standard, establishing patterns, mentoring engineers, and pushing for the design and architectural decisions that will shape Intercom’s frontend for years to come.You’ll solve highly complex problems that span the whole product development org, and ensure our frontend systems, frameworks, and practices scale with the business. You’ll drive technical direction, influence cross-functional strategy, and act as both a force multiplier and an expert executor, depending on the problem at hand.This is an opportunity to lead at the intersection of architecture, developer experience, and product enablement, while raising the bar for engineering across Intercom.This role is ideal for engineers who thrive at the intersection of technical depth, product impact, and leadership, and who want to raise the bar for the entire engineering organization. This role focuses on building and evolving the frontend platform, not on day-to-day feature development. If your experience and interests are mainly in developing product features, this may not be the right fit, but if you’re excited by shaping architecture, tooling, and developer experience at scale, you’ll thrive here.What will I be doing?As a Platform Engineer, You WillCollaborate with other world-class domain experts on the most complex and ambiguous projects, reducing risks early and ensuring execution quality.Develop new tools and services that make it fast, safe, and easy for other Intercom engineers to leverage our web frameworks.Participate in all phases of software development, including ideation, prototyping, design, and implementation.Play an active role in hiring, mentoring, and career development of other engineers.Raise the bar for technical standards, performance, reliability, and operational excellence.What skills do I need?3+ years of professional experience building high-quality web applications.Deep expertise in JavaScript, React, TypeScript, and modern frontend tooling.Strong foundation in web fundamentals (HTML, CSS, performance, accessibility) and the ability to teach and advocate for them.Strong track record of enabling other engineers or product teams, through frameworks, tooling, or architectural decisions.Proven ability to deliver on ambiguous projects, balancing speed, quality, and long-term maintainability.A product mindset: you understand the importance of velocity, usability, and customer impact when making engineering decisions.Experience building and shipping a design system.BenefitsWe are a well treated bunch, with awesome benefits! If there’s something important to you that’s not on this list, talk to us!Competitive salary and equity in a fast-growing start-upWe serve lunch every weekday, plus a variety of snack foods and a fully stocked kitchenRegular compensation reviews - we reward great workPension scheme & match up to 4%Peace of mind with life assurance, as well as comprehensive health and dental insurance for you and your dependentsFlexible paid time off policyPaid maternity leave, as well as 6 weeks paternity leave for fathers, to let you spend valuable time with your loved onesIf you’re cycling, we’ve got you covered on the Cycle-to-Work Scheme. With secure bike storage too MacBooks are our standard, but we also offer Windows for certain roles when needed.Policies Intercom has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week.We have a radically open and accepting culture at Intercom. We avoid spending time on divisive subjects to foster a safe and cohesive work environment for everyone. As an organization, our policy is to not advocate on behalf of the company or our employees on any social or political topics out of our internal or external communications. We respect personal opinion and expression on these topics on personal social platforms on personal time, and do not challenge or confront anyone for their views on non-work related topics. Our goal is to focus on doing incredible work to achieve our goals and unite the company through our core values.Intercom values diversity and is committed to a policy of Equal Employment Opportunity. Intercom will not discriminate against an applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law.
arr_raw: Intercom has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week.
label: maybe

title: Junior Full Stack PHP Software Engineer
jd_text: We’re looking for a UK-based Junior Full Stack Engineer to support development on our sustainability and digital learning platforms.You’ll be a curious and ambitious engineer who takes pride in writing clean, thoughtful code, enjoys solving real-world problems, and is excited to grow your skills while building technology that makes a meaningful impact.The Role at a Glance:Junior Full Stack EngineerOld Street, London / Hybrid Working / 1 day a week in the office£35,000Plus fantastic benefits: 8% employer pension contribution, mental health support, fitness allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity pay, volunteer days, and more…Permanent - Full TimeProduct / Service: Sustainability Consultancy; Training & EventsCulture: Close knit, small and agile company, flat hierarchy, direct communication with director and hiring managers alike** Heads-Up - We really value a short intro covering note :-) **Who we are:Action Sustainability is a leading consultancy with the aim of inspiring sustainable business.Our team comprises 60+ full and part-time employees and associates. Many of our clients are sector leaders on sustainability and supply chain issues, and we are proud of our reputation for leading the thinking in this area.Action Sustainability drives lasting change in sustainability through innovative technology, expert advisory, and award-winning learning solutions. They partner with leading organisations, including the NHS and Houses of Parliament, to create measurable impact across industries. As the delivery team behind the Supply Chain Sustainability School, we lead global collaboration to accelerate sustainability.An accredited Living Wage employer, we’ve been recognised by Best Companies with a 'One to Watch' status for their commitment to an outstanding workplace. We prioritise regular wellbeing check-ins, ensure employees are heard, and offer mental health support. Our competency framework enables employees to shape their career paths with clear development goals, while managers receive coaching, mentoring, and peer-sharing opportunities to enhance their leadership skills. This comprehensive approach supports an environment where personal and professional growth thrive.About the Role:You’ll work closely with senior engineers to build and improve features that impact thousands of learners, while developing your skills in modern web development and AI-assisted engineering.We make as much use of AI as possible and intend to expand its use so you’ll be encouraged to use AI tools responsibly to learn faster and ship better.Key Responsibilities:• Support the build and maintenance of custom Moodle plugins, themes and features (with guidance)• Write clean, reusable PHP and contribute to modern, responsive UI work (HTML/CSS/JS)• Assist with RESTful APIs and system integrations (implementing defined tasks)• Fix bugs, improve existing code, and contribute to small-to-medium enhancements• Write and maintain unit tests (and contribute to Behat tests where relevant)• Participate in code reviews as a learner (receiving feedback and making improvements)System Support & Quality• Help troubleshoot issues and improve performance under supervision• Follow best practices for accessibility, GDPR and data protection (with support)• Contribute to QA processes and data integrity checksCollaboration• Work within a cross-functional agile team (engineers, product designer, product manager)• Communicate progress clearly and ask for help early when blocked• Engage with stakeholders (e.g., Learning Technologists) mainly via your team leadAbout You:• English proficiency and UK work authorisation• 1–2 years commercial experience (or equivalent demonstrable experience/portfolio) in • • PHP/web developmentWorking Knowledge of:• PHP fundamentals• HTML, CSS3, JavaScript• Relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL)• GitDesirable:• Exposure to Moodle or other LMS platforms• Docker basics• Any experience using AI coding tools effectively• Familiarity with WordPressWhat’s on Offer:• A competitive market salary of £35,000• 25 days + 8 statutory holidays, plus an extra day for your birthday• Hybrid working• 8% employer pension contribution• If we exceed targets, the full team gets rewarded through our profit share scheme• Discretionary bonus• Fitness allowance• Enhanced maternity/paternity pay and childcare schemes• Volunteer days• Access to our vibrant Old Street office featuring a lounge, café bar, gym, and rooftop terrace• Leverage professional memberships and development programmes tailored to your career aspirations.Apply today and join a team that’s driving change, inspiring action, and leading the way in sustainable supply chains.Application notice... We take your privacy seriously. As you might expect you may be contacted by email, text or telephone. Your data is processed by our talent partner RR (Recruitment Revolution) on the basis of their legitimate interests in fulfilling the recruitment process. Please refer to their Data Privacy Policy & Notice on their website for further details.
arr_raw: Old Street, London / Hybrid Working / 1 day a week in the office
tech_raw: PHP, HTML, CSS3, JavaScript, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Git, Moodle, Docker, AI

title: Senior Software Engineer
jd_text: Role: Senior Software EngineerLocation: Canary Wharf, Central LondonSalary: £70,000-£75,000 Plus Hybrid / Flexible Working, Company Bonus, Generous Pension and more.We are working with a well-funded, mission-driven organisation in the renewable energy space looking to hire a Senior Software Engineer into a core engineering team.You’ll be working on complex, large-scale platforms supporting critical infrastructure at a national level.This is a high-impact engineering environment where strong technical input genuinely matters.You’ll be responsible not just for building software, but for contributing to technical direction, design decisions, and engineering standards across the team.Key ResponsibilitiesBuild and scale high-performance, cloud-based applicationsDevelop backend services using Python (Django)Work across TypeScript / JavaScript where neededContribute to technical design, architecture, and engineering best practicesProvide technical leadership, supporting and mentoring other engineersImprove system performance, scalability, and reliabilityCollaborate with Product, QA and DevOps to deliver featuresRequirementsStrong experience with Python (Django)Experience with JavaScript / TypeScriptExperience working in cloud environments (AWS, Azure or GCP)Strong understanding of APIs, microservices, and scalable systemsExperience contributing to technical design or architecture decisionsAbility to provide technical leadership and mentor othersExperience working in modern engineering environments (CI/CD, Agile)Desirable SkillsExperience with React, Node.js, Terraform or CeleryStrong database design and optimisation experienceIf you’re a Senior Engineer looking for a role combining hands-on development, technical ownership, and real-world impact, this is worth a conversation. Hit apply today!KeywordsSenior Software Engineer, Python Developer, Django, Flask, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, AWS, Azure, GCP, Cloud Engineer, Backend Developer, Full Stack Developer, Microservices, API Development, CI/CD, DevOps, Terraform, Celery, Data Engineering, Renewable Energy Tech, Technical Leadership, Software Architecture, Scalable Systems, Leeds Jobs, London Jobs, Hybrid Jobs, Tech Jobs UK
